verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein of the immunoglobulin superfamily expressed in sperm. In non-capacitated sperm, IZUMO1 is sequestered in an intracellular compartment (anterior head, overlying acrosome). Upon acrosome reaction (triggered during capacitation/fertilization), IZUMO1 rapidly translocates to the outer sperm plasma membrane surface, where it is essential for sperm-egg fusion via interaction with egg JUNO. Thus, IZUMO1 reaches the outer leaflet of the PM, but only in the context of the acrosome-reacted (activated) sperm state — a specific physiological cell-state transition.
